qt: The derivative of a zero order tensor becomes a …………. order tensor.
The covariant differentiation of a tensor with constant components is zero.
In generalized curvilinear coordinates the gradient of scalar function $\phi=\phi(x^{i})$ is defined as _______________.
The Christoffel symbols of first kind satisfy the transformation law of a third rank tensor.
The covariant derivative and partial derivatives coincide when __________ are constant.
In the notation $\Gamma_{ij}^{k}$ of Christoffel symbol of second, the symbol $k$ tells____________
op: which coordinate is being varied to cause a change in the basis vector
which basis vector points in the direction of this component of the derivative vector
which basis vector's change is being considered

qt: Which of the following expressions correctly defines the Christoffel symbols of the first kind?
Which of the following represents the Laplacian of a scalar field 𝜙 in curvilinear coordinates using covariant differentiation?
The covariant derivative of a tensor of order $n$ is a tensor of order __________.
The Christoffel symbols of first kind $\Gamma_{ijk}$ are symmetric in_____________.
For a diagonal metric which of the following is true?
In Cartesian coordinate system all the Christoffel symbols are _______________.
Which of the following is true for basis vectors in general curvilinear coordinates systems?
How are Christoffel symbols of first kind denoted?
The Christoffel symbols of second kind are symmetric in__________.
Which of the following represents the covariant derivative of a tensor $T_j^i$ with respect to ${x^k}$ in any coordinate system ${x^i}$?
Which of the following represents the covariant derivative of a contravariant vector ${T^i}$ with respect to ${x^k}$ in any coordinate system ${x^i}$?
Which of the following values represent the covariant derivative of kronecker delta $\delta _j^i$ with respect to coordinate ${x^k}$?
The ordinary derivative of a tensor is a tensor if and only if coordinate changes are restricted to __________________.
In any coordinate system $x^{i}$, the covariant derivative with respect to $x^{k}$ of a covariant vector $T_{i}$ is defined as______________
In generalized curvilinear coordinates the divergence of vector point function $A^{i}=A^{i}(x^{i})$ is defined as___________.
The Christoffel symbols of first and second kind are equal in any coordinate system.
Christoffel symbols of first and second kind include derivatives of ___________.
Which of the following represents the curl of a vector field 𝐴𝑖 in curvilinear coordinates using covariant differentiation?
The covariant differentiation of metric tensor is _____________________.
op: zero in rectangular Cartesian coordinates only

qt: The ordinary derivative of a tensor is a tensor in general.
In curvilinear coordinates the derivative of a vector also includes_____________________.
The covariant derivative of an arbitrary tensor is a tensor.
Christoffel symbols of first kind are related to second kind as ……………..
How Riemann tensor of second kind is converted into Riemann tensor of first kind?
op: Lowering two index with the metric tensor.
Lowering an index with the metric tensor.
Raising an index with the metric tensor.
Raising two index with the metric tensor.

qt: $${\text{Which of the following quantities are obtained, when }}\tau {\text{ - derivatives is replaced by }}t{\text{ - derivatives in the expressions of the velocity and acceleration}}?$$
op: Proper time and speed of light.

qt: Any 4 × 4 matrix that preserves the quadratic form ------------- is called the Lorentz?
Under which operation the set of Lorentz matrices form a group called the Lorentz group?
In a curvilinear coordinate system, the straight lines are called __________.
$${\text{In the inverse Lorentz form, the 4 }} \times {\text{ 4 matrix is typically represented as:}}$$
In Special Relativity, an event is defined as:
op: A group of events occurring together.
A path an object takes through space.
A specific location and time when something happens.

qt: The basic rules for differentiation from Calculus carry over to covariant differentiation but do not fully apply to absolute differentiation.
Which of the following expression shows the relation between Riemann tensor of first kind and second kind?
Which of the following is the correct mathematical form of the Bianchi identity?
Which expression is obtained by interchanging k and l in this term Rijkl ?
The acceleration in terms of metric tensor is defined as _________
Which of the following is true about an inertial frame of reference?
op: It moves with uniform velocity or remains at rest.

qt: How is the Ricci scalar computed?
op: By integrating the Ricci tensor over the manifold
By multiplying the metric tensor by the Riemann tensor
By contracting the Ricci tensor with the metric tensor

qt: The simple Lorentz matrix of 4 × 4 order is typically represented as:
Which of the following best describes the event space in Special Relativity?
op: A four-dimensional space combining three spatial dimensions and one time dimension.
A space where only spatial coordinates are considered.
A plane where events occur at the same time.
A three-dimensional space defined by an inertial observer.

qt: The Ricci tensor is obtained by contracting which tensor?
The arc length parameter is defined as ___________.
Which of the following is NOT an example of an inertial frame of reference?
op: A stationary observer on Earth ignoring Earth's rotation.
A car moving at constant velocity.
A spaceship in deep space far from any gravitational influence.

qt: Copositional events in Special Relativity have a spacelike interval.
$${\text{What is the expression for the relativistic mass }}\hat m{\text{ if }}m{\text{ is rest mass of the particle?}}$$
The matrix form of Minkowski spacetime is a _____________.
Which of the following is the correct mathematical form of the second skew symmetry of Riemann tensor?
For a lightlike interval:
The absolute derivative of $T^{i}$ along the curve $C: x^{i}=x^{i}(t)$ is defined as:
Minkowski spacetime is defined as:
The torsion is zero in the spaces where _________.
What is true for unit sphere?
If a flash of light occurs at (0, 0, 0) and time t = 0 which of the following equation describes the expanding spherical wave front?
What is a four vector in the special relativity?
op: A vector that only exists in non-relativistic physics.
A vector with three spatial components and one time component.
A vector with four spatial components.

qt: For a spacelike interval:
The curvature of the curve $C: x^{i}=x^{i}(t)$, is defined as ___________.
op: the norm of first order derivative of $x^{i}(s)$
the square root of first order derivative of $x^{i}(s)$
the norm of second order derivative of $x^{i}(s)$
the square root of second order derivative of $x^{i}(s)$

qt: The absolute derivative of $T^{i}$ along the curve $C: x^{i}=x^{i}(t)$ is denoted by ___________.
What happens to the length of an object when it moves close to the speed of light?
Simultaneous events in Special Relativity have a spacelike interval.
Which term is often chosen to reparameterize the proper time ?
What does the Lorentz transformation do?
op: It works for objects at rest.
It changes space-time coordinates between two moving frames.
